ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Specify the timeframe (in the past year, past two weeks, etc.) in which elements/items are assessed
Form used/assessment name
Notes: e.g., Self-Report, Parent Form, Short Form, Revised Version

ElementDescription: I eat breakfast.
Notes: 1= Less than once a week or never; 2= One day a week; 3= 2 to 3 days a week; 4= 4 to 5 days a week; 5= Every day of the week

ElementDescription: I get a good nights sleep, for example, uninterrupted, restful sleep.
Notes: 1= Less than once a week or never; 2= One day a week; 3= 2 to 3 days a week; 4= 4 to 5 days a week; 5= Every day of the week

ElementDescription: I drink 3 or more caffeinated beverages, such as coffee, tea or colas.
Notes: 1= Less than once a week or never; 2= One day a week; 3= 2 to 3 days a week; 4= 4 to 5 days a week; 5= Every day of the week

ElementDescription: I exercise for 20 continuous minutes or more, to the point of perspiration.
Notes: 1= Less than once a week or never; 2= One day a week; 3= 2 to 3 days a week; 4= 4 to 5 days a week; 5= Every day of the week

ElementDescription: I eat at least 3 meals a day.
Notes: 1= Less than once a week or never; 2= One day a week; 3= 2 to 3 days a week; 4= 4 to 5 days a week; 5= Every day of the week

ElementDescription: I take time to relax.
Notes: 1= Less than once a week or never; 2= One day a week; 3= 2 to 3 days a week; 4= 4 to 5 days a week; 5= Every day of the week

ElementDescription: I eat fresh fruits and/or vegetables.
Notes: 1= Less than once a week or never; 2= One day a week; 3= 2 to 3 days a week; 4= 4 to 5 days a week; 5= Every day of the week

ElementDescription: I walk as much as possible, for example, I take the stairs not the elevator, etc.
Notes: 1= Less than once a week or never; 2= One day a week; 3= 2 to 3 days a week; 4= 4 to 5 days a week; 5= Every day of the week

ElementDescription: I take vitamins.
Notes: 1= Less than once a week or never; 2= One day a week; 3= 2 to 3 days a week; 4= 4 to 5 days a week; 5= Every day of the week

ElementDescription: I eat junk foods, such as crisps, chips, sweets, Fresh fries, etc. (UK version) / chips, candy bars, French fries, etc. (US/Canadian version)
Notes: 1= Less than once a week or never; 2= One day a week; 3= 2 to 3 days a week; 4= 4 to 5 days a week; 5= Every day of the week

ElementDescription: I eat healthy, well balanced meals.
Notes: 1= Less than once a week or never; 2= One day a week; 3= 2 to 3 days a week; 4= 4 to 5 days a week; 5= Every day of the week

ElementDescription: I take natural supplements, such as garlic pills, Echinacea, herbals, etc.
Notes: 1= Less than once a week or never; 2= One day a week; 3= 2 to 3 days a week; 4= 4 to 5 days a week; 5= Every day of the week

ElementDescription: Wellness Behavior Inventory - 10-item Score (excluding vitamins and supplements)
